fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to missouri, speech-language pathologist compact missouri, missouri speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Advisory Commission for Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists
board.mailing_address:
Division of Professional Registration
P.O. Box 1335
Jefferson City, MO 65102-1335

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 30 hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Missouri.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Create an account and complete your application through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System (renew.pr.mo.gov/RenewSLP/Apply/Start.aspx). During this process, you will pay the non-refundable $50 application fee.

steps.title: Provide Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for all official transcripts from your graduate program, verifying your master's or doctoral degree in Speech-Language Pathology from an accredited institution, to be sent directly from your university to the Missouri Division of Professional Registration.

steps.title: Verify Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Ensure your Clinical Fellowship (Supervised Professional Experience) is verified by having your supervisor complete and submit the 'Speech-Language Pathologist Supervised Professional Experience Verification Form' directly to the Missouri Division of Professional Registration.

steps.title: Submit Praxis Exam Scores
steps.description: Request that your official scores for the national Speech-Language Pathology examination (Praxis II, exam #5331) be sent directly from ETS to the Missouri Division of Professional Registration. The ETS institutional code for Missouri is 7352.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System for any updates or requests for additional information from the Board. An initial licensure fee (prorated) will be required upon approval.

telepractice_text: Missouri permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Missouri issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Missouri is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri SLP license cost?
Is Missouri part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
Does Missouri allow telepractice for SLPs?
Does Missouri have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Missouri participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Missouri without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Missouri is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for an SLP license in Missouri, you must use the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System to submit your initial application and all required supporting documents.

official_links.portal_name: Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System
official_links.registration_steps: To start your application, visit the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System (pr.mo.gov/onlineapp.asp), click 'Apply for a New License', select 'Speech-Language Pathologist' from the list of professions, and then create an account or log in to proceed with your application.
